self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n {"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"call_095d6d94cea44067952f330a","type":"tool_result","content":"1\t\"\"\"\n2\tCluster → ArgoCD endpoint resolution (bead adc-1ejh).\n3\t\n4\tThere is no single ArgoCD API: which instance holds a project's Application\n5\tdepends on the cluster the project lives on. This module maps each cluster to\n6\tits ArgoCD API base URL and an `access` mode, loaded from\n7\t``config/clusters.yaml`` and hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache — the same\n8\tstrategy every hot-reloaded config artifact uses (stat the file each call,\n9\tre-read only when its mtime changed).\n10\t\n11\tThe fetch strand holds no ArgoCD credentials, so it can only *consume*\n12\t``read-only-proxy`` endpoints. Any cluster that is unmapped, or mapped to an\n13\taccess mode the strand cannot satisfy (``authenticated`` with no credential\n14\tpath), resolves as **unsatisfiable** — the ArgoCD source then fails with an\n15\thonest ``fetch_coverage`` caveat. This is what makes querying the wrong\n16\tinstance impossible: a wrong-instance query would otherwise return not-found\n17\t(indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\") and silently drop the sync row.\n18\t\n19\tSee docs/plan/plan.md → Fetch Strand → Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution.\n20\t\"\"\"\n21\t\n22\tfrom dataclasses import dataclass\n23\tfrom logging import getLogger\n24\tfrom pathlib import Path\n25\tfrom typing import Optional\n26\t\n27\timport yaml\n28\t\n29\tlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n30\t\n31\t# config/clusters.yaml lives at the repo root under config/; this file is\n32\t# src/fetch/clusters.py, so the repo root is three parents up.\n33\tCLUSTERS_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / \"config\" / \"clusters.yaml\"\n34\t\n35\t# The only access mode the fetch strand can satisfy: no credentials needed,\n36\t# a proxy injects a read-only token.\n37\tCONSUMABLE_ACCESS = \"read-only-proxy\"\n38\t\n39\t# mtime-checked cache (mirrors src/components/hot_reload.py's pattern).\n40\t_cache: Optional[dict] = None\n41\t_cache_mtime: Optional[float] = None\n42\t\n43\t\n44\tdef _read_clusters_file(path: Path = CLUSTERS_PATH) -> dict:\n45\t    \"\"\"Parse the raw YAML, returning the ``clusters:`` mapping (or {}).\"\"\"\n46\t    try:\n47\t        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text()) or {}\n48\t    except FileNotFoundError:\n49\t        logger.warning(f\"clusters config not found at {path}; all clusters unmapped\")\n50\t        return {}\n51\t    except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) as e:\n52\t        logger.error(f\"Failed to parse clusters config {path}: {e}\")\n53\t        # Preserve the previously-known-good cache rather than poisoning it:\n54\t        # return whatever is cached (possibly empty) so a malformed edit does\n55\t        # not flip every mapped cluster to unmapped mid-flight.\n56\t        return _cache or {}\n57\t    if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n58\t        logger.error(f\"clusters config {path} is not a mapping (got {type(raw).__name__})\")\n59\t        return _cache or {}\n60\t    clusters = raw.get(\"clusters\", {})\n61\t    if not isinstance(clusters, dict):\n62\t        logger.error(f\"'clusters' in {path} is not a mapping (got {type(clusters).__name__})\")\n63\t        return _cache or {}\n64\t    return clusters\n65\t\n66\t\n67\tdef get_clusters(force: bool = False) -> dict:\n68\t    \"\"\"\n69\t    Return the parsed ``clusters`` mapping, reloading only when the file's\n70\t    mtime has changed (or when ``force`` is set, for tests).\n71\t\n72\t    A benign parse failure returns the last good cache instead of an empty\n73\t    dict, so a transiently-malformed edit never silently unmaps everything.\n74\t    \"\"\"\n75\t    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n76\t    try:\n77\t        mtime = CLUSTERS_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n78\t    except OSError:\n79\t        # File vanished: keep the last-known-good cache (best effort).\n80\t        return _cache or {}\n81\t\n82\t    if force or _cache is None or mtime != _cache_mtime:\n83\t        parsed = _read_clusters_file()\n84\t        # Don't poison the cache on a parse failure: _read_clusters_file\n85\t        # already returns the prior cache in that case, so this is a no-op\n86\t        # when parsing failed — but guard explicitly for clarity.\n87\t        _cache = parsed\n88\t        _cache_mtime = mtime\n89\t        logger.debug(f\"Reloaded clusters config ({len(parsed)} clusters) from {CLUSTERS_PATH}\")\n90\t    return _cache\n91\t\n92\t\n93\tdef reset_cache() -> None:\n94\t    \"\"\"Clear the mtime cache (test hook).\"\"\"\n95\t    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n96\t    _cache = None\n97\t    _cache_mtime = None\n98\t\n99\t\n100\t@dataclass(frozen=True)\n101\tclass ArgocdResolution:\n102\t    \"\"\"Result of resolving a cluster to an ArgoCD endpoint.\n103\t\n104\t    ``satisfiable`` is True only when the strand can actually issue the query —\n105\t    i.e. the cluster is mapped AND its access mode is consumable without\n106\t    credentials. When False, ``reason`` explains why (for the caveat strip).\n107\t    \"\"\"\n108\t\n109\t    cluster: Optional[str]\n110\t    argocd_api: Optional[str]\n111\t    access: Optional[str]\n112\t    satisfiable: bool\n113\t    reason: Optional[str] = None\n114\t\n115\t\n116\tdef resolve_argocd_endpoint(cluster: Optional[str]) -> ArgocdResolution:\n117\t    \"\"\"\n118\t    Resolve a cluster name to a consumable ArgoCD API base URL.\n119\t\n120\t    Resolution rules (every unsatisfiable case carries a human-readable\n121\t    ``reason`` for the fetch_coverage caveat):\n122\t\n123\t    * ``cluster`` is None/empty → unsatisfiable (\"no cluster configured …\")\n124\t    * cluster absent from clusters.yaml → unsatisfiable (\"no ArgoCD mapping …\")\n125\t    * access == ``read-only-proxy`` → satisfiable, argocd_api set\n126\t    * access == ``authenticated`` → unsatisfiable (\"requires authentication …\")\n127\t    * any other/unknown access mode → unsatisfiable (\"unsupported access mode …\")\n128\t    \"\"\"\n129\t    if not cluster:\n130\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n131\t            cluster=None,\n132\t            argocd_api=None,\n133\t            access=None,\n134\t            satisfiable=False,\n135\t            reason=\"No cluster configured for this project; ArgoCD endpoint cannot be resolved\",\n136\t        )\n137\t\n138\t    entry = get_clusters().get(cluster)\n139\t    if entry is None:\n140\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n141\t            cluster=cluster,\n142\t            argocd_api=None,\n143\t            access=None,\n144\t            satisfiable=False,\n145\t            reason=(\n146\t                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' has no ArgoCD mapping in config/clusters.yaml; \"\n147\t                f\"ArgoCD source omitted to avoid querying the wrong instance\"\n148\t            ),\n149\t        )\n150\t\n151\t    access = entry.get(\"access\")\n152\t    argocd_api = entry.get(\"argocd_api\")\n153\t\n154\t    if access == CONSUMABLE_ACCESS:\n155\t        if not argocd_api:\n156\t            return ArgocdResolution(\n157\t                cluster=cluster,\n158\t                argocd_api=None,\n159\t                access=access,\n160\t                satisfiable=False,\n161\t                reason=f\"Cluster '{cluster}' is read-only-proxy but defines no argocd_api\",\n162\t            )\n163\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n164\t            cluster=cluster,\n165\t            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n166\t            access=access,\n167\t            satisfiable=True,\n168\t        )\n169\t\n170\t    if access == \"authenticated\":\n171\t        return ArgocdResolution(\n172\t            cluster=cluster,\n173\t            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n174\t            access=access,\n175\t            satisfiable=False,\n176\t            reason=(\n177\t                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' ArgoCD requires authentication \"\n178\t                f\"(no no-auth read-only proxy available); This module maps each cluster to\nits ArgoCD API base URL and an `access` mode, loaded from\n``config/clusters.yaml`` and hot-reloaded via an mtime-checked cache — the same\nstrategy every hot-reloaded config artifact uses (stat the file each call,\nre-read only when its mtime changed).\n\nThe fetch strand holds no ArgoCD credentials, so it can only *consume*\n``read-only-proxy`` endpoints. Any cluster that is unmapped, or mapped to an\naccess mode the strand cannot satisfy (``authenticated`` with no credential\npath), resolves as **unsatisfiable** — the ArgoCD source then fails with an\nhonest ``fetch_coverage`` caveat. This is what makes querying the wrong\ninstance impossible: a wrong-instance query would otherwise return not-found\n(indistinguishable from \"app doesn't exist\") and silently drop the sync row.\n\nSee docs/plan/plan.md → Fetch Strand → Cluster→ArgoCD Endpoint Resolution.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom dataclasses import dataclass\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom pathlib import Path\nfrom typing import Optional\n\nimport yaml\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n# config/clusters.yaml lives at the repo root under config/; this file is\n# src/fetch/clusters.py, so the repo root is three parents up.\nCLUSTERS_PATH = Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent.parent / \"config\" / \"clusters.yaml\"\n\n# The only access mode the fetch strand can satisfy: no credentials needed,\n# a proxy injects a read-only token.\nCONSUMABLE_ACCESS = \"read-only-proxy\"\n\n# mtime-checked cache (mirrors src/components/hot_reload.py's pattern).\n_cache: Optional[dict] = None\n_cache_mtime: Optional[float] = None\n\n\ndef _read_clusters_file(path: Path = CLUSTERS_PATH) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"Parse the raw YAML, returning the ``clusters:`` mapping (or {}).\"\"\"\n    try:\n        raw = yaml.safe_load(path.read_text()) or {}\n    except FileNotFoundError:\n        logger.warning(f\"clusters config not found at {path}; all clusters unmapped\")\n        return {}\n    except (yaml.YAMLError, OSError) as e:\n        logger.error(f\"Failed to parse clusters config {path}: {e}\")\n        # Preserve the previously-known-good cache rather than poisoning it:\n        # return whatever is cached (possibly empty) so a malformed edit does\n        # not flip every mapped cluster to unmapped mid-flight.\n        return _cache or {}\n    if not isinstance(raw, dict):\n        logger.error(f\"clusters config {path} is not a mapping (got {type(raw).__name__})\")\n        return _cache or {}\n    clusters = raw.get(\"clusters\", {})\n    if not isinstance(clusters, dict):\n        logger.error(f\"'clusters' in {path} is not a mapping (got {type(clusters).__name__})\")\n        return _cache or {}\n    return clusters\n\n\ndef get_clusters(force: bool = False) -> dict:\n    \"\"\"\n    Return the parsed ``clusters`` mapping, reloading only when the file's\n    mtime has changed (or when ``force`` is set, for tests).\n\n    A benign parse failure returns the last good cache instead of an empty\n    dict, so a transiently-malformed edit never silently unmaps everything.\n    \"\"\"\n    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n    try:\n        mtime = CLUSTERS_PATH.stat().st_mtime\n    except OSError:\n        # File vanished: keep the last-known-good cache (best effort).\n        return _cache or {}\n\n    if force or _cache is None or mtime != _cache_mtime:\n        parsed = _read_clusters_file()\n        # Don't poison the cache on a parse failure: _read_clusters_file\n        # already returns the prior cache in that case, so this is a no-op\n        # when parsing failed — but guard explicitly for clarity.\n        _cache = parsed\n        _cache_mtime = mtime\n        logger.debug(f\"Reloaded clusters config ({len(parsed)} clusters) from {CLUSTERS_PATH}\")\n    return _cache\n\n\ndef reset_cache() -> None:\n    \"\"\"Clear the mtime cache (test hook).\"\"\"\n    global _cache, _cache_mtime\n    _cache = None\n    _cache_mtime = None\n\n\n@dataclass(frozen=True)\nclass ArgocdResolution:\n    \"\"\"Result of resolving a cluster to an ArgoCD endpoint.\n\n    ``satisfiable`` is True only when the strand can actually issue the query —\n    i.e. the cluster is mapped AND its access mode is consumable without\n    credentials. When False, ``reason`` explains why (for the caveat strip).\n    \"\"\"\n\n    cluster: Optional[str]\n    argocd_api: Optional[str]\n    access: Optional[str]\n    satisfiable: bool\n    reason: Optional[str] = None\n\n\ndef resolve_argocd_endpoint(cluster: Optional[str]) -> ArgocdResolution:\n    \"\"\"\n    Resolve a cluster name to a consumable ArgoCD API base URL.\n\n    Resolution rules (every unsatisfiable case carries a human-readable\n    ``reason`` for the fetch_coverage caveat):\n\n    * ``cluster`` is None/empty → unsatisfiable (\"no cluster configured …\")\n    * cluster absent from clusters.yaml → unsatisfiable (\"no ArgoCD mapping …\")\n    * access == ``read-only-proxy`` → satisfiable, argocd_api set\n    * access == ``authenticated`` → unsatisfiable (\"requires authentication …\")\n    * any other/unknown access mode → unsatisfiable (\"unsupported access mode …\")\n    \"\"\"\n    if not cluster:\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=None,\n            argocd_api=None,\n            access=None,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=\"No cluster configured for this project; ArgoCD endpoint cannot be resolved\",\n        )\n\n    entry = get_clusters().get(cluster)\n    if entry is None:\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=None,\n            access=None,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=(\n                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' has no ArgoCD mapping in config/clusters.yaml; \"\n                f\"ArgoCD source omitted to avoid querying the wrong instance\"\n            ),\n        )\n\n    access = entry.get(\"access\")\n    argocd_api = entry.get(\"argocd_api\")\n\n    if access == CONSUMABLE_ACCESS:\n        if not argocd_api:\n            return ArgocdResolution(\n                cluster=cluster,\n                argocd_api=None,\n                access=access,\n                satisfiable=False,\n                reason=f\"Cluster '{cluster}' is read-only-proxy but defines no argocd_api\",\n            )\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n            access=access,\n            satisfiable=True,\n        )\n\n    if access == \"authenticated\":\n        return ArgocdResolution(\n            cluster=cluster,\n            argocd_api=argocd_api,\n            access=access,\n            satisfiable=False,\n            reason=(\n                f\"Cluster '{cluster}' ArgoCD requires authentication \"\n                f\"(no no-auth read-only proxy available); ArgoCD source omitted\"\n            ),\n        )\n\n    return ArgocdResolution(\n        cluster=cluster,\n        argocd_api=argocd_api,\n        access=access,\n        satisfiable=False,\n        reason=(\n            f\"Cluster '{cluster}' uses unsupported ArgoCD access mode '{access}' \"\n            f\"(fetch strand can only consume '{CONSUMABLE_ACCESS}')\"\n        ),\n    )\n\n\nclass ArgocdEndpointUnresolvable(Exception):\n    \"\"\"Raised when an ArgoCD query cannot be issued without risking the wrong instance.\n\n    Carries the human-readable resolution ``reason`` so the orchestrator can\n    surface it as a fetch_coverage caveat. executor\nto test dispatch/parallelism/coverage, this suite exercises the **real executor\nimplementations** in src/fetch/orchestrator.py against a mocked HTTP layer —\nproving that each fetch source type (kubectl API, ArgoCD API, pod logs, events)\nparses its real response shape into the structured dict synthesize consumes.\n\nhttpx is faked, so no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, or live cluster is hit.\nNo subprocess or filesystem source is touched here (those are covered by the\nlocal-repo integration test at the end).\n\nWhat this suite locks down (the \"various fetch source types execute correctly\"\ncontract):\n\n1. **HTTP source types parse real payloads** — kubectl_pods, kubectl_deployments,\n   kubectl_workflows, argocd_app, logs, and events each turn a realistic API\n   response into the field set the component library expects.\n2. **Missing-argument guards** — every executor returns a structured error dict\n   (and makes no HTTP call) when its required context fields are absent, rather\n   than raising.\n3. **HTTP error handling** — 404s and empty-result envelopes become error dicts,\n   not exceptions (synthesize surfaces caveats, never crashes).\n4. **End-to-end strand wiring** — a real FetchStrand.fetch() over a STATUS\n   utterance, with httpx mocked and local sources stubbed, yields parsed data\n   for each HTTP source — i.e. the strand correctly maps FetchContext, FetchRequest, FetchSource, IntentType\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchStrand\n\n# --- httpx fakes -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FakeResponse:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for httpx.Response — serves canned JSON/text + status.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n        self._json_data = json_data\n        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n        self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n                response=self,\n            )\n\n\nclass FakeAsyncClient:\n    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, responder):\n        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n        self._responder = responder\n        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n        return False\n\n    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n        self.requests.append((url, params))\n        if callable(self._responder):\n            return self._responder(url, params)\n        return self._responder\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"\n    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n    that constructs one gets `responder`. Returns the installed client so tests\n    can assert on which URLs/params were hit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def install(responder) -> FakeAsyncClient:\n        client = FakeAsyncClient(responder)\n        # Executors construct via `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=..., verify=...)`;\n        # ignore those kwargs and hand back our instrumented client.\n        monkeypatch.setattr(orchestrator.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", lambda *a, **kw: client)\n        return client\n\n    return install\n\n\ndef _ctx(**kwargs) -> FetchContext:\n    \"\"\"FetchContext with sane kubectl-proxy defaults + per-test overrides.\"\"\"\n    defaults = {\n        \"proxy\": \"http://traefik-test:8001\",\n        \"namespace\": \"myns\",\n        \"project_slug\": \"my-proj\",\n    }\n    defaults.update(kwargs)\n    return FetchContext(**defaults)\n\n\n# --- 1. src/fetch/orchestrator.py against a mocked HTTP layer —\nproving that each fetch source type (kubectl API, ArgoCD API, pod logs, events)\nparses its real response shape into the structured dict synthesize consumes.\n\nhttpx is faked, so no kubectl proxy, ArgoCD endpoint, or live cluster is hit.\nNo subprocess or filesystem source is touched here (those are covered by the\nlocal-repo integration test at the end).\n\nWhat this suite locks down (the \"various fetch source types execute correctly\"\ncontract):\n\n1. **HTTP source types parse real payloads** — kubectl_pods, kubectl_deployments,\n   kubectl_workflows, argocd_app, logs, and events each turn a realistic API\n   response into the field set the component library expects.\n2. **Missing-argument guards** — every executor returns a structured error dict\n   (and makes no HTTP call) when its required context fields are absent, rather\n   than raising.\n3. **HTTP error handling** — 404s and empty-result envelopes become error dicts,\n   not exceptions (synthesize surfaces caveats, never crashes).\n4. **End-to-end strand wiring** — a real FetchStrand.fetch() over a STATUS\n   utterance, with httpx mocked and local sources stubbed, yields parsed data\n   for each HTTP source — i.e. the strand correctly maps source types to\n   executors to structured results.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom unittest.mock import MagicMock\n\nimport httpx\nimport pytest\n\nfrom src.fetch import orchestrator\nfrom src.fetch.commands import FetchContext, FetchRequest, FetchSource, IntentType\nfrom src.fetch.orchestrator import FetchStrand\n\n# --- httpx fakes -----------------------------------------------------------\n\n\nclass FakeResponse:\n    \"\"\"Stand-in for httpx.Response — serves canned JSON/text + status.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n        self._json_data = json_data\n        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n        self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n                response=self,\n            )\n\n\nclass FakeAsyncClient:\n    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, responder):\n        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n        self._responder = responder\n        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n        return False\n\n    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n        self.requests.append((url, params))\n        if callable(self._responder):\n            return self._responder(url, params)\n        return self._responder\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"\n    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n    that constructs one gets `responder`. Returns the installed client so tests\n    can assert on which URLs/params were hit.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def install(responder) -> FakeAsyncClient:\n        client = FakeAsyncClient(responder)\n        # Executors construct via `httpx.AsyncClient(timeout=..., verify=...)`;\n        # ignore those kwargs and hand back our instrumented client.\n        monkeypatch.setattr(orchestrator.httpx, \"AsyncClient\", lambda *a, **kw: client)\n        return client\n\n    return install\n\n\ndef _ctx(**kwargs) -> FetchContext:\n    \"\"\"FetchContext with sane kubectl-proxy defaults + per-test overrides.\"\"\"\n    defaults = {\n        \"proxy\": \"http://traefik-test:8001\",\n        \"namespace\": \"myns\",\n        \"project_slug\": \"my-proj\",\n    }\n    defaults.update(kwargs)\n    return FetchContext(**defaults)\n\n\n# --- 1. status.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, json_data=None, text=\"\", status_code=200):\n        self._json_data = json_data\n        self.text = text if text else \"\"\n        self.status_code = status_code\n\n    def json(self):\n        if self._json_data is None:\n            raise ValueError(\"response has no JSON body\")\n        return self._json_data\n\n    def raise_for_status(self):\n        if self.status_code >= 400:\n            raise httpx.HTTPStatusError(\n                f\"HTTP {self.status_code}\",\n                request=MagicMock(),\n                response=self,\n            )\n\n\nclass FakeAsyncClient:\n    \"\"\"async-with fake for httpx.AsyncClient. Routes GETs via `responder`.\"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, responder):\n        # responder: either a FakeResponse, or a callable(url, params) -> FakeResponse\n        self._responder = responder\n        self.requests: list[tuple[str, dict | None]] = []\n\n    async def __aenter__(self):\n        return self\n\n    async def __aexit__(self, *args):\n        return False\n\n    async def get(self, url, params=None):\n        self.requests.append((url, params))\n        if callable(self._responder):\n            return self._responder(url, params)\n        return self._responder\n\n\n@pytest.fixture\ndef mock_httpx(monkeypatch):\n    \"\"\"\n    Patch httpx.AsyncClient in the orchestrator module so the next executor\n    that constructs one gets `responder`.
